nodes.label: GENESIS System
nodes.rationale: Split into a Python Neuromorphic Physics Engine backend and a Vanilla-JS Observation Deck frontend, connected over a single WebSocket (ws://<host>:8085); earlier opcode/graph engines (turing_engine.py, genesis_engine.py, DynamicBrain) were deleted in favour of the genome-encoded SNN engine.

nodes.label: neuromorphic_engine.py
nodes.rationale: The hot path: an SNN simulator compiled to machine code with Numba @njit(cache=True) for speed; holds the memory allocator, genome decoder, sensory encoder and world_tick_numba.

nodes.label: genesis_lab.py
nodes.rationale: Owns all global state (NumPy arrays), the deep-time sim_loop, reproduction/mutation, the Elite Ark, and the asyncio/websockets server.

nodes.label: The Universe (RAM Substrate)
nodes.rationale: Space is literal RAM: a 1D toroidal uint8 array of RAM_SIZE=65536 bytes (g_ram). Position is a byte address, movement is pointer arithmetic mod RAM_SIZE; food is byte 0x55, curriculum/Oracle text occupies printable ASCII 32-126.

nodes.label: The Global Heap
nodes.rationale: No per-organism matrices: neurons, synapses and genomes for the whole universe live in flat global arrays and each organism mallocs contiguous blocks via malloc_block/free_block. Models the universe's total computational matter as one shared heap, so memory fragmentation is itself a spatial hazard.

nodes.label: Genome-Encoded SNN Organism
nodes.rationale: A genome is a byte string parsed into three record types (receptor, neuron, synapse); every organism has a fixed I/O layer of N_INPUT=15 sensory + N_OUTPUT=14 motor neurons plus variable hidden neurons.

nodes.label: Receptor Protein (RECEPTOR_MARKER 195)
nodes.rationale: A 10-byte DNA-encoded plasticity profile (A_PLUS, A_MINUS, TAU_P, TAU_M, V_REST, V_RESET, TAU_DEFAULT, SPIKE_RATE_MAX); up to MAX_RECEPTORS_PER_ORG=16 per organism.

nodes.label: Hidden Neuron (NEURON_MARKER 162)
nodes.rationale: A 5-byte record expressing a receptor id, a threshold and a leak time-constant.

nodes.label: Synapse Gene (GENE_MARKER 161)
nodes.rationale: A 4-byte synapse record (src, dst, weight) where weight maps a raw byte to float(w)-128, i.e. -128..+127.

nodes.label: Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Model
nodes.rationale: float32 voltage: leak v += (v_rest - v)/tau, fire when v >= threshold, reset to v_reset, refractory TAU_REF=1 step; input neurons fire stochastically proportional to sensed value x SPIKE_RATE_MAX.

nodes.label: STDP (Hebbian Plasticity)
nodes.rationale: On a post-synaptic spike, pre-before-post potentiates (w += A_PLUS·e^(-Δt/TAU_P)) and pre-after-post depresses (w -= A_MINUS·e^(-Δt/TAU_M)); weights clamp to [W_MIN, W_MAX] = [-128, 127].

nodes.label: Baldwin-style In-Lifetime Learning
nodes.rationale: Lifetime STDP changes are held in the runtime weight array; behavioural adaptation acquired within a lifetime is not itself directly inherited (contrast with the Lamarckian 50/50 blend).

nodes.label: Lamarckian Consolidation
nodes.rationale: At reproduction the birth path (neuromorphic_engine.py ~L498-521) re-walks the child's copied genome like decode_genome and overwrites each hidden/output synapse weight byte with a 50/50 blend of the parent's initial DNA weight and the weight the parent learned via STDP this lifetime (global_conn_weight, re-encoded +128), making acquired plasticity partially heritable. The 50/50 constants are hardcoded inline (a Rule 17 target for DNA-encoding). Corrects a stale earlier claim that learned weights were never written back.

nodes.label: Frontend Observation Deck
nodes.rationale: Vanilla-JS frontend (public/) rendering the 65536-byte RAM as a folded 256x256 HTML5 canvas; includes KPI tiles, an extinction chart, a D3 Brain Analyzer that decompiles the Elite genome's synapses, an Oracle Terminal, and a Library of Genesis curriculum injector.

nodes.label: Sensors (15) and Motors (14)
nodes.rationale: Inputs: energy reserve, bias, crowding, RAM byte under pointer, neighbours' vocal-cord bits, and the 8 bits of the Oracle broadcast (7-14). Outputs: jumps +/-1 and +/-10, consume, reproduce, and 8 vocal-cord bits (emitted ASCII character).

nodes.label: Thermodynamics = CPU Cycles
nodes.rationale: Energy is execution cycles (ATP_MAX=1,000,000 ceiling) drained per action at honest raw-cycle counts (Rule 15/17): synapse transmission 1, neuron membrane update 1×n_neurons/step, activity-gated STDP update 1, movement 3, viscosity stall n_neurons; consume +1024. Neuron update was formerly discounted to 0.1× (brain size ~10× too cheap), so billing at true rate makes lean brains out-reproduce costly ones — Rule 7 efficiency selected emergently by thermodynamics, never a fitness metric. Costs are activity-gated to reward many sparsely-firing synapses (the 20 W paradigm, Rule 11).

nodes.label: Computational Viscosity (Rule 13)
nodes.rationale: Local code density in a ±16-byte window sets a stall probability (up to 0.5) each tick; stalled steps burn maintenance energy but do no computation, penalising dense genomes and favouring sparse topologies.

nodes.label: Conservation of Compute
nodes.rationale: Per-tick LIF step budget is a global pool: steps = GLOBAL_CYCLE_POOL(3000)/population; mass replication dilutes everyone's compute, coupling population to a fixed energy budget.

nodes.label: Cosmic Radiation (Entropy)
nodes.rationale: Two random bit-flips per tick applied inside living organisms' genomes (germline mutation) rather than the mostly-empty 5 MB arena where ~99% of flips previously landed in vacuum. Known limitation: phenotype is decoded once at spawn, so a flip alters offspring not the running brain — true in-lifetime somatic entropy is future work.

nodes.label: The Elite Ark (Rule 14)
nodes.rationale: sim_loop continuously tracks the highest-age living genome; on total extinction the universe is reseeded (300 organisms) from mutated copies of that Elite, guaranteeing strictly ascending evolution. The Elite genome is checkpointed to Brain/Brain.npz.

nodes.label: Reproduction & Mutation
nodes.rationale: Reproduce pays genome_length×CYCLES_PER_BYTE_COPY then splits remaining energy 50/50 with the child; organism dies at energy<=0. mutate_dna applies insertion/deletion/gene-duplication (5% each) else point mutations at rate 1/genome_length; receptor-marker bytes 0-1 are protected so a lineage cannot lose all STDP in one flip.

nodes.label: Oracle Uplink & Curriculum
nodes.rationale: User can broadcast an 8-bit character (sensed on inputs 7-14), read back the Elite's vocal-cord output, and inject ASCII books as byte patterns into the substrate — a still-human-in-the-loop scaffold toward the Autotelic Imperative (Rule 9).

nodes.label: The Autotelic Imperative (Rule 9)
nodes.rationale: The long-term goal of a self-directed, self-teaching system that removes the human-in-the-loop curriculum scaffold.

nodes.label: Numba @njit(cache=True)
nodes.rationale: Ahead-of-time cached JIT compilation to machine code, used to make the SNN hot path fast.

nodes.label: elite_iq Dashboard Metric
nodes.rationale: elite_iq = age / footprint is observation-only and must never feed back into selection (Rule 5/9); efficiency must remain emergent from thermodynamics, not an imposed fitness metric.
